title: codex canary harness + A/B tiny protocol (GH #22 P1-7, D9)
action: scripts/canary_codex.mjs: skip-guarded (no codex binary -> exit 0 'skipped'); per-run mktemp fixture repo (repo-copy install of bee) AND a per-run CODEX_HOME mktemp dir so --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust and every codex config/trust write stays inside the throwaway (advisor R7 — never touch ~/.codex); drive codex exec --ephemeral --json inside the fixture; assert: (1) SessionStart hook fired, (2) unmarked spawn_agent denied by the guard, (3) marked spawn allowed, (4) update_plan triggers state-sync, (5) pre-Gate-3 source write blocked by the write guard; cleanup fixture + CODEX_HOME in finally; unique paths per run (concurrent-safe). .github/workflows/canary.yml: workflow_dispatch + nightly cron; ubuntu; optional codex install step clearly marked; job reports 'skipped' cleanly when codex unavailable; never a push gate. docs/decisions/ab-tiny-protocol.md: arms (inline-with-cell-discipline vs one-dispatched-worker) for codex tiny cells; metrics wired to the g22-2 economics fields + perf log (wall time, time-to-first-edit, tool calls, tokens); sample-size + read-out rule; where results are recorded; explicitly defers data to real usage (no synthetic benchmark). Run canary locally against real codex 0.144.4 — green or name the exact failing probe.

truths: canary runs green locally against real codex 0.144.4 (or names the exact failing probe)
canary is skip-guarded and side-effect-free outside its temp fixture (trust bypass only inside)
canary.yml is manual/nightly and never gates pushes
A/B protocol doc defines arms, metrics (wired to the g22-2 economics fields), and read-out rule, honestly deferring data to real usage

trace.outcome: Skip-guarded real-codex canary green on 0.144.4 (isolated CODEX_HOME, project-trust finding pinned), nightly workflow non-gating, A/B tiny protocol doc shipped
trace.verification_evidence:
# g22-6 — done report (codex canary + A/B tiny protocol)

Worker: g22-6-worker (sonnet, isolated worktree, rebased onto 20642b1).
Worker commit `9584df5`, merged into main. 2026-07-19.

## What shipped
- `scripts/canary_codex.mjs`: skip-guarded real-codex smoke suite. Per-run
  mktemp CODEX_HOME + mktemp fixture repo, both cleaned in finally (advisor
  R7 — the bypass flag's trust writes never touch ~/.codex). Fixture built
  via `onboard_bee.mjs --apply --repo-hooks --runtime codex` (deliberately
  NOT install.sh, which could touch real plugin CLIs — documented). Probes:
  P1 real `codex exec -C <fixture> --json --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust`
  → session-init artifact created; P2-P5 synthetic envelopes piped through
  the fixture's INSTALLED hook files (spawn guard deny/allow, state-sync,
  write-guard intake deny) — honest labels per probe.
- Load-bearing findings pinned in the script header: (1) the bypass flag
  bypasses per-HOOK trust only — per-PROJECT trust must be seeded in the
  throwaway CODEX_HOME's config.toml or hooks silently no-op; (2) `codex
  exec` needs `-C <fixture>` explicitly.
- `.github/workflows/canary.yml`: workflow_dispatch + nightly cron; succeeds
  with "skipped" when codex unavailable; never a push gate.
- `docs/decisions/ab-tiny-protocol.md`: inline-vs-worker arms for codex tiny
  cells, metrics wired to perf log + g22-2 economics fields, assignment +
  sample-size + read-out rules; data accrues from real usage.

## Verification (fresh command output)
Independent orchestrator run on main post-merge (real codex-cli 0.144.4):
P1-P5 all PASS, "canary: all probes green" (~9s). Worker also verified the
skip guard (PATH without codex → skipped, exit 0) and YAML parse.

## Diff
3 files changed, 408 insertions(+).
